 {"id":"bv-3fcg","title":"Light Mode Terminal: Low Color Contrast Makes Text Unreadable","description":"## Problem Statement

The bv color scheme is very hard to read on light terminal backgrounds. The 'dimmed' text (Subtext, Muted colors) is nearly invisible on white backgrounds, making the application unusable for users with light terminal themes.

## User Impact

**Severity: High (P1)**
- Effectively blocks users with light terminal themes
- Affects readability of secondary information (dimmed text, hints)
- Reported by @mikker in GitHub Issue #17

## Root Cause Analysis

**Location:** `pkg/ui/theme.go:41-64`

The theme uses `lipgloss.AdaptiveColor` which provides Light/Dark values:

```go
Subtext: lipgloss.AdaptiveColor{Light: "#999999", Dark: "#BFBFBF"}
Muted:   lipgloss.AdaptiveColor{Light: "#888888", Dark: "#6272A4"}
```

The Light values are too light for white backgrounds:
- `#999999` on white = contrast ratio ~2.8:1 (WCAG AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text)
- `#888888` on white = contrast ratio ~3.5:1 (still fails WCAG AA)

While lipgloss attempts auto-detection of terminal background color, the chosen Light values are simply too faint regardless.

## Proposed Solution

### Fix 1: Improve Light mode color contrast

```go
// Improved light mode colors (WCAG AA compliant)
Subtext: lipgloss.AdaptiveColor{Light: "#666666", Dark: "#BFBFBF"} // ~6:1 contrast
Muted:   lipgloss.AdaptiveColor{Light: "#555555", Dark: "#6272A4"} // ~7:1 contrast
```

All colors need review:
- Primary: #7D56F4 on white = ~4.6:1 (barely passes)
- Secondary: #555555 is good
- Status colors need verification

### Fix 2 (Optional): Add explicit theme flag

```bash
bv --theme=light   # Force light mode colors
bv --theme=dark    # Force dark mode colors
bv --theme=auto    # Default: auto-detect (current behavior)
```

This helps when auto-detection fails or user preference differs.

## Color Audit Required

All AdaptiveColor values need contrast verification:

| Color | Current Light | Contrast | Suggested Light | New Contrast |
|-------|--------------|----------|-----------------|--------------|
| Subtext | #999999 | 2.8:1 ❌ | #666666 | 6:1 ✓ |
| Muted | #888888 | 3.5:1 ❌ | #555555 | 7:1 ✓ |
| Secondary | #555555 | 7:1 ✓ | Keep | - |
| Open | #00A800 | 2.7:1 ❌ | #007700 | 4.6:1 ✓ |
| Closed | #555555 | 7:1 ✓ | Keep | - |
| Border | #DDDDDD | 1.4:1 ❌ | #AAAAAA | 2.3:1 (border OK) |

## Test Plan

1. Manual test on iTerm2 with Solarized Light theme
2. Manual test on macOS Terminal with default light theme
3. Verify all text elements are readable
4. Verify status colors are distinguishable
5. Consider adding screenshot tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All text readable on white terminal background
- [ ] Status colors visually distinguishable on light background
- [ ] Existing dark mode appearance unchanged
- [ ] Optional: --theme flag for explicit control
- [ ] No WCAG AA violations for text colors (4.5:1 minimum)

## References

- GitHub Issue #17: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer/issues/17
- WCAG Color Contrast Guidelines: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/contrast-minimum.html
- lipgloss AdaptiveColor: https://github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss#adaptive-colors","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T04:52:19.518335Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T05:22:39.249340Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T05:22:39.249340Z","close_reason":"Improved light mode color contrast for WCAG AA compliance - darker Subtext, Muted, Open, InProgress, Feature, Task, Chore, and Border colors","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["accessibility","gh-issue-17"]}
 {"id":"bv-3ht4","title":"openInEditor Uses Hardcoded Path Instead of Configured beadsPath","description":"## Problem Statement

The 'O' keybinding to open the beads file in editor fails when the file isn't named exactly `beads.jsonl`. The `openInEditor()` function hardcodes the path instead of using the `beadsPath` field already stored in the Model.

## User Impact

**Severity: High (P1)**
- Fails silently or shows error for projects using `issues.jsonl`
- beads upstream uses `issues.jsonl` as canonical, not `beads.jsonl`
- Reported by @qmx in GitHub PR #18

## Root Cause Analysis

**Location:** `pkg/ui/model.go:2945-2955`

```go
func (m *Model) openInEditor() {
    cwd, err := os.Getwd()
    // ...
    beadsFile := filepath.Join(cwd, ".beads", "beads.jsonl")  // HARDCODED!
    if _, err := os.Stat(beadsFile); os.IsNotExist(err) {
        m.statusMsg = "No .beads/beads.jsonl file found"  // Wrong error message
        // ...
    }
}
```

The Model already has a `beadsPath` field (line 138) that's set during initialization and used for file watching. This function ignores it.

## Why This Matters

The beads project documentation (pre-commit hook) states:
> Stage all tracked JSONL files (issues.jsonl is canonical, beads.jsonl for backward compat)

So the hardcoded `beads.jsonl` path is wrong for the canonical case.

## Proposed Solution

```go
func (m *Model) openInEditor() {
    if m.beadsPath == "" {
        m.statusMsg = "No beads file configured"
        m.statusIsError = true
        return
    }

    if _, err := os.Stat(m.beadsPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
        m.statusMsg = fmt.Sprintf("Beads file not found: %s", m.beadsPath)
        m.statusIsError = true
        return
    }
    
    // Continue with m.beadsPath instead of hardcoded path...
}
```

## Test Plan

1. Test: openInEditor with beadsPath set to issues.jsonl - should work
2. Test: openInEditor with beadsPath empty - should show "No beads file configured"
3. Test: openInEditor with beadsPath pointing to non-existent file - should show path in error
4. Manual test: 'O' key works for both beads.jsonl and issues.jsonl projects

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Uses m.beadsPath instead of hardcoded path
- [ ] Error messages include actual file path for debugging
- [ ] Works for issues.jsonl, beads.jsonl, and any other valid jsonl file
- [ ] Tests cover all error paths

## Dependencies

**Blocked by:** bv-mvvu (Data Integrity: Merge Artifact Files)
- The beadsPath value depends on correct file selection
- Fix the loader first, then this will use the correct path

## References

- GitHub PR #18: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer/pull/18
- beads pre-commit hook documentation on canonical file names","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T04:52:16.050304Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T05:20:26.597794Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T05:20:26.597794Z","close_reason":"Fixed: openInEditor now uses m.beadsPath instead of hardcoded 'beads.jsonl'.  {"id":"bv-bikt","title":"Port Critical Path Heights to Rust WASM","description":"# Port Critical Path Heights to Rust WASM

## Context
Critical path heights measure the longest dependency chain from roots to each node. Nodes with high heights are deep in the dependency tree.

## Go Implementation Reference
```go
// computeHeights in graph.go
// DAG depth using topological order
```

## Rust Implementation (critical_path.rs)
```rust
use crate::graph::DiGraph;
use crate::algorithms::topo::topological_sort;

/// Compute critical path heights (depth in DAG).
/// Height[v] = 1 + max(height of predecessors)
pub fn critical_path_heights(graph: &DiGraph) -> Vec<f64> {
    let n = graph.node_count();
    if n == 0 {
        return Vec::new();
    }
    
    // Topological order (returns None if cyclic)
    let order = match topological_sort(graph) {
        Some(o) => o,
        None => return vec![0.0; n], // Return zeros for cyclic graphs
    };
    
    let mut heights = vec![0.0; n];
    
    // Process in topological order
    for &v in &order {
        let max_pred_height = graph.predecessors(v)
            .iter()
            .map(|&u| heights[u])
            .fold(0.0, f64::max);
        
        heights[v] = 1.0 + max_pred_height;
    }
    
    heights
}

/// Get nodes on the critical path (those with maximum height).
pub fn critical_path_nodes(graph: &DiGraph) -> Vec<usize> {
    let heights = critical_path_heights(graph);
    if heights.is_empty() {
        return Vec::new();
    }
    
    let max_height = heights.iter().cloned().fold(0.0, f64::max);
    heights.iter()
        .enumerate()
        .filter(|(_, &h)| (h - max_height).abs() < 0.001)
        .map(|(i, _)| i)
        .collect()
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Heights computed correctly for DAGs
- [ ] Returns zeros for cyclic graphs
- [ ] Critical path nodes identified","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T04:34:44.599253Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T05:19:34.579579Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T05:19:34.579579Z","close_reason":"Implemented critical_path_heights, critical_path_nodes, and critical_path_length functions.  {"id":"bv-bjv0","title":"Cass Result Caching System","description":"# Cass Result Caching System

## Purpose
Avoid redundant cass searches by caching correlation results. Critical for performance—without caching, navigating the issue list would trigger many slow external calls.

## Background

### Why Cache?
- cass searches take 50-500ms each
- Users navigate between beads frequently
- Same bead is often selected multiple times
- Correlation results change slowly (sessions rarely deleted)

### Cache Requirements
- Fast lookup (sub-ms)
- Bounded memory (max 100 entries)
- Automatic expiration (10-minute TTL)
- Thread-safe (concurrent UI access)
- LRU eviction (keep most recently used)

## Implementation

### Cache Structure
```go
type CacheEntry struct {
    Hint      *CorrelationHint
    CachedAt  time.Time
    ExpiresAt time.Time
}

type Cache struct {
    entries map[string]*CacheEntry  // key = bead ID
    order   []string                 // LRU order (newest at end)
    maxSize int
    ttl     time.Duration
    mu      sync.RWMutex
}
```

### Cache Operations
```go
// Get returns cached hint or nil if miss/expired
func (c *Cache) Get(beadID string) *CorrelationHint

// Set stores a correlation hint
func (c *Cache) Set(beadID string, hint *CorrelationHint)

// Invalidate removes a specific entry
func (c *Cache) Invalidate(beadID string)

// Clear removes all entries
func (c *Cache) Clear()

// Stats returns cache statistics (for debugging)
func (c *Cache) Stats() CacheStats
```

### Eviction Policy
When cache is full:
1. Remove expired entries first
2. If still full, remove least recently used
3. Never exceed maxSize

### Cache Key Design
Simple: use bead ID directly (`"bv-abc123"`)

Could extend later with workspace prefix if supporting multiple projects.

## Configuration
```go
const (
    DefaultCacheSize = 100       // Max entries
    DefaultCacheTTL  = 10 * time.Minute
)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LRU eviction works correctly
- [ ] TTL expiration works
- [ ] Thread-safe for concurrent access
- [ ] Get/Set operations are O(1) for hit
- [ ] Memory bounded by maxSize
- [ ] Clear operation works

## Testing Strategy
- Test cache hit/miss
- Test TTL expiration
- Test LRU eviction order
- Test concurrent access (race detector)
- Benchmark operations","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T20:49:14.815057Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T21:40:17.399001Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T21:40:17.399001Z","close_reason":"Implemented LRU cache with TTL expiration, bounded memory (100 entries max), O(1) Get/Set, thread-safe concurrent access. 19 cache tests pass with -race.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}

 {"id":"bv-bxoe","title":"Implement DiGraph core structure with adjacency lists","description":"# Implement DiGraph Core Structure

## Context
The DiGraph struct is the foundation of the WASM graph engine. It needs to efficiently store directed graphs and support fast traversal for all algorithms.

## Requirements

### Core Structure (graph.rs)
```rust
use wasm_bindgen::prelude::*;
use std::collections::HashMap;

/// Directed graph optimized for graph algorithms.
/// Uses adjacency lists for O(1) neighbor access.
#[wasm_bindgen]
pub struct DiGraph {
    /// Node ID strings (issue IDs like "bv-123")
    nodes: Vec<String>,
    
    /// Reverse lookup: ID string -> index
    node_index: HashMap<String, usize>,
    
    /// Forward adjacency: adj[u] = vec of nodes that u points to
    /// (u depends on these nodes)
    adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>,
    
    /// Reverse adjacency: rev_adj[v] = vec of nodes pointing to v
    /// (these nodes depend on v)
    rev_adj: Vec<Vec<usize>>,
    
    /// Edge count (for density calculation)
    edge_count: usize,
}

#[wasm_bindgen]
impl DiGraph {
    /// Create an empty graph
    #[wasm_bindgen(constructor)]
    pub fn new() -> DiGraph {
        DiGraph {
            nodes: Vec::new(),
            node_index: HashMap::new(),
            adj: Vec::new(),
            rev_adj: Vec::new(),
            edge_count: 0,
        }
    }
    
    /// Create a graph with pre-allocated capacity
    pub fn with_capacity(node_capacity: usize, edge_capacity: usize) -> DiGraph {
        DiGraph {
            nodes: Vec::with_capacity(node_capacity),
            node_index: HashMap::with_capacity(node_capacity),
            adj: Vec::with_capacity(node_capacity),
            rev_adj: Vec::with_capacity(node_capacity),
            edge_count: 0,
        }
    }
    
    /// Add a node, returns its index. Idempotent - returns existing index if already present.
    pub fn add_node(&mut self, id: &str) -> usize {
        if let Some(&idx) = self.node_index.get(id) {
            return idx;
        }
        let idx = self.nodes.len();
        self.nodes.push(id.to_string());
        self.node_index.insert(id.to_string(), idx);
        self.adj.push(Vec::new());
        self.rev_adj.push(Vec::new());
        idx
    }
    
    /// Add a directed edge from -> to. Idempotent.
    pub fn add_edge(&mut self, from: usize, to: usize) {
        // Check bounds
        if from >= self.nodes.len() || to >= self.nodes.len() {
            return; // Silently ignore invalid edges
        }
        
        // Check if edge already exists (linear scan is fine for typical degree)
        if self.adj[from].contains(&to) {
            return;
        }
        
        self.adj[from].push(to);
        self.rev_adj[to].push(from);
        self.edge_count += 1;
    }
    
    /// Number of nodes
    pub fn node_count(&self) -> usize {
        self.nodes.len()
    }
    
    /// Number of edges
    pub fn edge_count(&self) -> usize {
        self.edge_count
    }
    
    /// Get node ID by index
    pub fn node_id(&self, idx: usize) -> Option<String> {
        self.nodes.get(idx).cloned()
    }
    
    /// Get node index by ID
    pub fn node_idx(&self, id: &str) -> Option<usize> {
        self.node_index.get(id).copied()
    }
    
    /// Get all node IDs as JSON array
    pub fn node_ids(&self) -> JsValue {
        JsValue::from_serde(&self.nodes).unwrap_or(JsValue::NULL)
    }
    
    /// Out-degree of a node (number of dependencies)
    pub fn out_degree(&self, node: usize) -> usize {
        self.adj.get(node).map_or(0, |v| v.len())
    }
    
    /// In-degree of a node (number of dependents)
    pub fn in_degree(&self, node: usize) -> usize {
        self.rev_adj.get(node).map_or(0, |v| v.len())
    }
    
    /// All out-degrees as a vector
    pub fn out_degrees(&self) -> Vec<usize> {
        self.adj.iter().map(|v| v.len()).collect()
    }
    
    /// All in-degrees as a vector
    pub fn in_degrees(&self) -> Vec<usize> {
        self.rev_adj.iter().map(|v| v.len()).collect()
    }
}

// Internal methods (not exposed to WASM)
impl DiGraph {
    /// Get successors of a node (internal use)
    pub(crate) fn successors(&self, node: usize) -> &[usize] {
        self.adj.get(node).map_or(&[], |v| v.as_slice())
    }
    
    /// Get predecessors of a node (internal use)
    pub(crate) fn predecessors(&self, node: usize) -> &[usize] {
        self.rev_adj.get(node).map_or(&[], |v| v.as_slice())
    }
    
    /// Iterate over all edges (internal use)
    pub(crate) fn edges(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = (usize, usize)> + '_ {
        self.adj.iter().enumerate().flat_map(|(from, tos)| {
            tos.iter().map(move |&to| (from, to))
        })
    }
}
```

### Memory Layout Considerations
- **Vec<Vec<usize>>**: Good cache locality for typical graphs
- **HashMap for lookup**: O(1) ID→index conversion
- **Reverse adjacency**: Pre-computed for fast predecessor queries
- **No edge weights**: All algorithms assume unit weights (suitable for dependencies)

### Serialization Support
```rust
use serde::{Serialize, Deserialize};

#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct GraphSnapshot {
    pub nodes: Vec<String>,
    pub edges: Vec<(usize, usize)>,
}

impl DiGraph {
    /// Export graph as JSON snapshot
    pub fn to_json(&self) -> String {
        let snapshot = GraphSnapshot {
            nodes: self.nodes.clone(),
            edges: self.edges().collect(),
        };
        serde_json::to_string(&snapshot).unwrap_or_default()
    }
    
    /// Import graph from JSON snapshot
    pub fn from_json(json: &str) -> Result<DiGraph, String> {
        let snapshot: GraphSnapshot = serde_json::from_str(json)
            .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
        
        let mut graph = DiGraph::with_capacity(snapshot.nodes.len(), snapshot.edges.len());
        for id in snapshot.nodes {
            graph.add_node(&id);
        }
        for (from, to) in snapshot.edges {
            graph.add_edge(from, to);
        }
        Ok(graph)
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DiGraph struct compiles with wasm-bindgen
- [ ] add_node is idempotent
- [ ] add_edge is idempotent
- [ ] Degree queries work correctly
- [ ] Serialization round-trips correctly
- [ ] Unit tests pass for all methods
- [ ] Memory usage is reasonable (<10 bytes per edge)

## Why This Design?
1. **Adjacency lists**: O(degree) neighbor iteration, O(1) edge existence check for small degree
2. **Reverse adjacency**: Essential for PageRank, reachability, predecessor queries
3. **String IDs**: Keep issue IDs human-readable, avoid index confusion
4. **No edge data**: Keeps structure simple, all our algorithms use unit weights

 {"id":"bv-k79d","title":"Implement WASM memory management and object lifecycle","description":"# WASM Memory Management

## Context
Rust WASM objects allocated via wasm-bindgen are NOT automatically garbage collected by JavaScript. Each DiGraph, subgraph, or result object must be explicitly freed to prevent memory leaks.

## Requirements

### 1. Implement .free() on all exported types
wasm-bindgen automatically generates .free() methods, but we need to:
- Document their usage
- Ensure all exported types support it
- Add defensive checks

### 2. JavaScript Patterns for Memory Safety
```javascript
// BAD: Memory leak
function analyze() {
    const subgraph = GRAPH.subgraph(indices);
    return subgraph.pagerank(0.85, 100);
    // subgraph never freed!
}

// GOOD: Explicit cleanup
function analyze() {
    const subgraph = GRAPH.subgraph(indices);
    try {
        return subgraph.pagerank(0.85, 100);
    } finally {
        subgraph.free();
    }
}

// BETTER: Helper wrapper
function withSubgraph(indices, fn) {
    const subgraph = GRAPH.subgraph(indices);
    try {
        return fn(subgraph);
    } finally {
        subgraph.free();
    }
}
```

### 3. Alpine.js Store Cleanup
```javascript
Alpine.store('graph', {
    instance: null,
    destroy() {
        if (this.instance) {
            this.instance.free();
            this.instance = null;
        }
    }
});

// Call on page unload
window.addEventListener('beforeunload', () => {
    Alpine.store('graph').destroy();
});
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All DiGraph methods documented re: memory
- [ ] Helper functions for safe subgraph usage
- [ ] Cleanup on page unload
- [ ] No memory leaks in 10-minute usage session","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T04:52:20.137545Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:44.819631Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:44.819631Z","close_reason":"Implemented withSubgraph helper for automatic cleanup, cleanupWasm for page unload, fixed memory leak in recalculateMetrics, added diagnostics panel with live memory stats","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["integration","phase-4","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bv-k79d","depends_on_id":"bv-y7a0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-16T04:52:28.262133Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bv-kdn","title":"Implement gh CLI integration for GitHub Pages deployment","description":"# Implement gh CLI Integration for GitHub Pages Deployment

## Context
Integration with GitHub's `gh` CLI tool to create repositories, push content, and enable GitHub Pages - matching the approach used in mcp_agent_mail.

## Requirements

### Detection and Installation
```go
// pkg/export/github.go

func CheckGHInstalled() (bool, error) {
    _, err := exec.LookPath("gh")
    return err == nil, nil
}

func CheckGHAuthenticated() (bool, string, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("gh", "auth", "status")
    output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    // Parse output for username
    return err == nil, parseUsername(output), nil
}

func InstallGH() error {
    // Detect package manager
    switch detectPackageManager() {
    case "brew":
        return exec.Command("brew", "install", "gh").Run()
    case "apt":
        // Show manual instructions
        return fmt.Errorf("Run: curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/...")
    default:
        return fmt.Errorf("Install gh CLI from: https://cli.github.com/")
    }
}

func AuthenticateGH() error {
    cmd := exec.Command("gh", "auth", "login")
    cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
    cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    return cmd.Run()
}
```

### Repository Operations
```go
func CreateRepository(name string, private bool, description string) (string, error) {
    visibility := "--public"
    if private {
        visibility = "--private"
    }
    
    cmd := exec.Command("gh", "repo", "create", name,
        visibility,
        "--description", description,
        "--clone=false",
    )
    
    if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
        return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create repository: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Get full repo name (owner/repo)
    return getRepoFullName(name)
}

func getRepoFullName(name string) (string, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("gh", "repo", "view", name,
        "--json", "nameWithOwner",
        "-q", ".nameWithOwner",
    )
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    return strings.TrimSpace(string(output)), err
}

func RepoExists(name string) bool {
    cmd := exec.Command("gh", "repo", "view", name)
    return cmd.Run() == nil
}
```

### Git Operations
```go
func InitAndPush(bundlePath string, repoFullName string) error {
    // Initialize git repo
    cmds := [][]string{
        {"git", "init"},
        {"git", "add", "."},
        {"git", "commit", "-m", "Initial static site export"},
        {"git", "branch", "-M", "main"},
        {"git", "remote", "add", "origin", 
            fmt.Sprintf("https://github.com/%s.git", repoFullName)},
        {"git", "push", "-u", "origin", "main", "--force"},
    }
    
    for _, args := range cmds {
        cmd := exec.Command(args[0], args[1:]...)
        cmd.Dir = bundlePath
        if output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("%s failed: %s", args[0], output)
        }
    }
    
    return nil
}
```

### Enable GitHub Pages
```go
func EnableGitHubPages(repoFullName string) (string, error) {
    // Enable Pages via API
    cmd := exec.Command("gh", "api",
        fmt.Sprintf("repos/%s/pages", repoFullName),
        "-X", "POST",
        "-f", "source[branch]=main",
        "-f", "source[path]=/",
    )
    
    if output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
        // Check if already enabled
        if strings.Contains(string(output), "already exists") {
            // Pages already enabled, get URL
            return getGitHubPagesURL(repoFullName)
        }
        return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to enable Pages: %s", output)
    }
    
    // Wait for Pages to be configured
    time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
    
    return getGitHubPagesURL(repoFullName)
}

func getGitHubPagesURL(repoFullName string) (string, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("gh", "api",
        fmt.Sprintf("repos/%s/pages", repoFullName),
        "-q", ".html_url",
    )
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        // Construct URL manually as fallback
        parts := strings.Split(repoFullName, "/")
        return fmt.Sprintf("https://%s.github.io/%s/", parts[0], parts[1]), nil
    }
    return strings.TrimSpace(string(output)), nil
}
```

### High-Level Deploy Function
```go
type GitHubDeployConfig struct {
    RepoName        string
    Private         bool
    Description     string
    BundlePath      string
}

func DeployToGitHubPages(config GitHubDeployConfig) (string, error) {
    // 1. Check/install gh CLI
    if installed, _ := CheckGHInstalled(); !installed {
        if err := InstallGH(); err != nil {
            return "", err
        }
    }
    
    // 2. Check/perform authentication
    if authed, _, _ := CheckGHAuthenticated(); !authed {
        if err := AuthenticateGH(); err != nil {
            return "", err
        }
    }
    
    // 3. Create repository (or use existing)
    repoFullName, err := CreateRepository(config.RepoName, config.Private, config.Description)
    if err != nil {
        if RepoExists(config.RepoName) {
            repoFullName, _ = getRepoFullName(config.RepoName)
        } else {
            return "", err
        }
    }
    
    // 4. Initialize git and push
    if err := InitAndPush(config.BundlePath, repoFullName); err != nil {
        return "", err
    }
    
    // 5. Enable GitHub Pages
    pagesURL, err := EnableGitHubPages(repoFullName)
    if err != nil {
        return "", err
    }
    
    return pagesURL, nil
}
```

 {"id":"bv-kdug","title":"Implement Reachability queries (from/to) in Rust WASM","description":"# Implement Reachability Queries

## Context
Reachability queries find all nodes reachable from/to a given node. Essential for impact analysis and dependency exploration.

## Rust Implementation (reachability.rs)
```rust
use crate::graph::DiGraph;
use std::collections::VecDeque;

/// Find all nodes reachable from source (BFS forward).
pub fn reachable_from(graph: &DiGraph, source: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    let n = graph.node_count();
    if source >= n {
        return Vec::new();
    }
    
    let mut visited = vec![false; n];
    let mut queue = VecDeque::new();
    let mut result = Vec::new();
    
    queue.push_back(source);
    visited[source] = true;
    
    while let Some(v) = queue.pop_front() {
        result.push(v);
        for &w in graph.successors(v) {
            if !visited[w] {
                visited[w] = true;
                queue.push_back(w);
            }
        }
    }
    
    result
}

/// Find all nodes that can reach target (BFS backward).
pub fn reachable_to(graph: &DiGraph, target: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    let n = graph.node_count();
    if target >= n {
        return Vec::new();
    }
    
    let mut visited = vec![false; n];
    let mut queue = VecDeque::new();
    let mut result = Vec::new();
    
    queue.push_back(target);
    visited[target] = true;
    
    while let Some(v) = queue.pop_front() {
        result.push(v);
        for &u in graph.predecessors(v) {
            if !visited[u] {
                visited[u] = true;
                queue.push_back(u);
            }
        }
    }
    
    result
}

/// Get direct blockers (predecessors) of a node.
pub fn blockers(graph: &DiGraph, node: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    graph.predecessors(node).to_vec()
}

/// Get direct dependents (successors) of a node.
pub fn dependents(graph: &DiGraph, node: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
    graph.successors(node).to_vec()
}

/// Check if all predecessors of node are in closed_set.
pub fn is_actionable(graph: &DiGraph, node: usize, closed_set: &[bool]) -> bool {
    graph.predecessors(node).iter().all(|&p| {
        closed_set.get(p).copied().unwrap_or(false)
    })
}

/// Get all actionable nodes (no open blockers).
pub fn actionable_nodes(graph: &DiGraph, closed_set: &[bool]) -> Vec<usize> {
    (0..graph.node_count())
        .filter(|&i| !closed_set.get(i).copied().unwrap_or(false))
        .filter(|&i| is_actionable(graph, i, closed_set))
        .collect()
}
```

### WASM Bindings
```rust
#[wasm_bindgen]
impl DiGraph {
    pub fn reachable_from(&self, source: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
        reachability::reachable_from(self, source)
    }
    
    pub fn reachable_to(&self, target: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
        reachability::reachable_to(self, target)
    }
    
    pub fn blockers(&self, node: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
        reachability::blockers(self, node)
    }
    
    pub fn dependents(&self, node: usize) -> Vec<usize> {
        reachability::dependents(self, node)
    }
    
    pub fn actionable_nodes(&self, closed_set: &[u8]) -> Vec<usize> {
        let closed: Vec<bool> = closed_set.iter().map(|&b| b != 0).collect();
        reachability::actionable_nodes(self, &closed)
    }
}
```

2. Johnson's algorithm for full cycle enumeration

### Part 2: Johnson's Algorithm (enumerate cycles)
```rust
/// Enumerate elementary cycles using Johnson's algorithm.
/// Reference: Donald B. Johnson, 'Finding All Elementary Circuits'
pub fn enumerate_cycles(graph: &DiGraph, max_cycles: usize) -> Vec<Vec<usize>> {
    let n = graph.node_count();
    if n == 0 { return Vec::new(); }
    
    let mut cycles = Vec::new();
    let mut blocked = vec![false; n];
    let mut blocked_map: Vec<HashSet<usize>> = vec![HashSet::new(); n];
    let mut stack = Vec::new();
    
    // Unblock helper
    fn unblock(u: usize, blocked: &mut [bool], blocked_map: &mut [HashSet<usize>]) {
        blocked[u] = false;
        for w in blocked_map[u].drain() {
            if blocked[w] {
                unblock(w, blocked, blocked_map);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Circuit search from start vertex
    fn circuit(
        v: usize, start: usize, graph: &DiGraph,
        blocked: &mut [bool], blocked_map: &mut [HashSet<usize>],
        stack: &mut Vec<usize>, cycles: &mut Vec<Vec<usize>>,
        max_cycles: usize, active: &[bool],
    ) -> bool {
        if cycles.len() >= max_cycles { return false; }
        
        let mut found = false;
        stack.push(v);
        blocked[v] = true;
        
        for &w in graph.successors(v) {
            if !active[w] { continue; }
            
            if w == start {
                // Found a cycle
                cycles.push(stack.clone());
                found = true;
            } else if !blocked[w] {
                if circuit(w, start, graph, blocked, blocked_map, 
                          stack, cycles, max_cycles, active) {
                    found = true;
                }
            }
        }
        
        if found {
            unblock(v, blocked, blocked_map);
        } else {
            for &w in graph.successors(v) {
                if active[w] {
                    blocked_map[w].insert(v);
                }
            }
        }
        
        stack.pop();
        found
    }
    
    // Run Johnson's algorithm
    let mut active = vec![true; n];
    for start in 0..n {
        if cycles.len() >= max_cycles { break; }
        
        // Reset blocked state
        blocked.iter_mut().for_each(|b| *b = false);
        blocked_map.iter_mut().for_each(|s| s.clear());
        
        circuit(start, start, graph, &mut blocked, &mut blocked_map,
               &mut stack, &mut cycles, max_cycles, &active);
        
        active[start] = false;
    }
    
    cycles
}
```
